title: ChaosMachineInput
description: Schema for chaos-machine experiment input passed to the Step Functions state machine

testDescription.description: Human-readable description of what this experiment tests

experimentTemplateId.description: AWS FIS ExperimentTemplate ID (starts with EXT)

manifest.description: Operator-owned experiment metadata describing scenario class, ownership, rollback path, and evidence expectations

class.description: Scenario family such as dependency-failure, network-disruption, overload, recovery-validation, infrastructure-disruption, or data-consistency

healthLayer.description: Health layer under test: liveness, local, dependency, or anomaly

environment.description: Approved execution environment such as dev-aws, staging-like, or production-approved

expectedResultClass.description: Expected result classification such as steady-state-preserved, degraded-but-contained, or recovery-validated

contact.description: Operator contact handle or escalation reference

tier.description: Approval tier such as tier-1, tier-2, or tier-3

approvedBy.description: Named approvers or approval roles for the scenario

runbook.description: Runbook or document reference for rollback

summary.description: Short operator summary of the rollback path

destination.description: Planned destination for experiment evidence and findings

retention.description: Evidence retention expectation or review window

stopConditionAlarmArns.description: CloudWatch alarm ARNs used as FIS stop conditions (blast-radius guardrails)

steadyStateMetrics.description: CloudWatch metric definitions for steady-state verification. Use e* IDs for expression verdicts (0=FAIL, 1=PASS), m* for raw input data.

steadyStateAlarms.description: CloudWatch alarm names to check as part of steady-state verification

recoveryDelay.description: Seconds after experiment ends before starting recovery metric evaluation

recoveryDuration.description: Duration in seconds of the recovery evaluation window
